old_state: The locked protocol requires public/source strata to be separated from generated stress witnesses.
new_state: Six official documentation/example slices plus one generated canonical law probe are recorded in source_manifest.csv and public_subjects_manifest.csv.
rationale: Close corpus-depth and provenance risk while preserving the denominator rule that rejected and unsupported rows are not counted.
result_handling: All quantitative claims derive from the evaluated run and claim_manifest.json; rejected and unsupported rows remain outside the pass denominator.

old_state: The protocol requires ablation and scalability axes to be explicit before paper-facing use.
new_state: Nineteen ablation rows and fifty-seven scalability-axis rows are recorded across the locked axes.
rationale: Make negative/control evidence visible without treating deterministic scalability plumbing as a performance claim.
result_handling: No favorable-result filtering; ledgers report completed axes and no timing or performance advantage claim is made.

old_state: Paper-facing counts require a machine-checkable binding to result ledgers.
new_state: paper/claim_macros.tex and artifact/results/paper_claims.csv bind paper-facing counts to artifact/results/claim_manifest.json and verify_full_claims.py.
rationale: Prevent paper/artifact count drift.
result_handling: No metric is introduced; this change strengthens traceability of existing evidence.

old_state: The OPA target must not enter the denominator unless a vetted executable is available before decisions are observed.
new_state: OPA exclusion accounting is recorded before counted decisions; counted OPA execution is governed by the later hash-gated OPA closure row.
rationale: Prevent overclaiming and make adapter exclusion auditable.
result_handling: OPA rows are counted only in the hash-gated run described by AM-023.

old_state: Candidate constructors must not decide denominator status by themselves.
new_state: Main evaluation computes candidate status through executable predicate functions and emits predicate_evaluations.csv plus soundness_checks.csv.
rationale: Make the core novelty first-class in the artifact rather than a paper-only label.
result_handling: Counts are recomputed from predicate-computed statuses; rejected and unsupported rows remain outside the pass denominator.

old_state: Public subjects need native raw-fixture evidence in addition to normalized slices.
new_state: Six native-format public fixture files under subjects/native_public and three normalized public native-import subjects are recorded.
rationale: Deepen public evidence provenance and make public-to-canonical translation auditable.
result_handling: Primary counts are recomputed over public, native, generated, and stress strata; paper-facing counts derive from claim_manifest.json.

old_state: OPA execution requires a pre-result pinning contract.
new_state: The OPA/Rego CLI adapter and upstream checksum contract are implemented; counted OPA rows require the hash-gated run in AM-023.
rationale: Move OPA from target intent to executable adapter path without silently counting an unavailable binary.
result_handling: OPA pass/fail rows are counted only after the AM-023 hash-gated run.

old_state: Standalone replay requires artifact-local claim snapshots and explicit Python-wheel constraints.
new_state: Added artifact-local claim_macros_snapshot.tex fallback, Python 3.13 vendored-wheel requirement, predicate verifier, preflight verifier, and anonymity/hygiene verifier.
rationale: Improve standalone replay and anonymity checks.
result_handling: No metric change; verifier coverage is expanded.

old_state: Row-level generated candidates need a separate finite semantic audit.
new_state: Bounded deny-overrides RBAC model checking over all relation families writes model_check_cases.csv and model_check_summary.json.
rationale: Strengthen the theory-facing contribution and catch over-broad applicability predicates before adapter execution.
result_handling: Adapter pass denominator is unchanged; model-check counts are reported separately and verified.

old_state: Native raw fixtures must be executed before normalization evidence is trusted.
new_state: native_selftest_results.csv records raw Casbin/Cedar fixture execution before normalization.
rationale: Ensure native fixture provenance is executable evidence rather than only file provenance.
result_handling: Primary obligation denominator is unchanged; self-tests are reported and verified separately.

old_state: Theory and research-question evidence must be exposed as first-class ledgers.
new_state: Added theorem_ledger.csv, theorem_obligations.csv, research_questions.csv, novelty_matrix.csv, research_quality_matrix.csv, and dedicated verifiers.
rationale: Strengthen theory depth and paper-evidence traceability without changing the primary adapter denominator.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; proof and quality ledgers are separately verified and included in the hash ledger.

old_state: Quality challenges need executable closure evidence and robust direct script replay.
new_state: Added quality-challenge ledgers, paper impact matrix, an aggregate gate, an extra unit test, structural hash memoization, and package-path script execution for run_full_evaluation.py.
rationale: Convert multi-lens quality closure into first-class evidence and make replay entry points robust.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; aggregate-check and unit-test counts increase through verifier-backed checks.

old_state: Counterexample replay must check seeded-mutant semantics rather than only object shape.
new_state: Added semantic_counterexample_replay.csv/json, independent seeded-mutant replay logic, before/after request preservation, and corrected PA implication semantics.
rationale: Ensure minimized counterexamples are semantically replayable and remove unsound positive-control inflation.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; seeded killed rows and minimized counterexamples are recomputed and verified.

old_state: Theory and baseline-efficacy evidence should not be scattered across prose and raw ledgers.
new_state: Added mechanized_law_kernel.csv/json, oracle_efficacy_summary.csv/json, research_quality_gate_matrix.csv/json, and corresponding verifiers/tests.
rationale: Strengthen theory depth, baseline interpretation, and denominator-safety explanation.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; law-kernel and oracle-efficacy evidence are reported separately.

old_state: The replay package needs explicit conference-readiness, package manifest, generated-fixture exclusion, and direct replay termination checks.
new_state: Added venue_requirements_check.csv/json, artifact_integrity_check.json, artifact_manifest.csv, create_artifact_bundle.py, verify_venue_requirements.py, verify_artifact_integrity_check.py, generated-fixture exclusion from the sync manifest, and direct run_full_evaluation termination hardening.
rationale: Close package bloat, replay observability, and manifest-consistency risks.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; package and conformance checks are verifier-backed.

old_state: Counted adapter decisions need a direct bridge to reference semantics, and bibliography integrity needs an executable gate.
new_state: Added adapter_reference_agreement.csv/json, reference_integrity_gate.csv/json, dedicated verifiers/tests, and in-memory real-adapter replay material hashes.
rationale: Close adapter-wrapper translation and bibliography-integrity attack surfaces without changing primary denominators.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; generated adapter fixture by-products are not materialized during primary replay.

old_state: Package hygiene, PDF shape, claim traceability, source provenance, adapter/reference agreement, and reference integrity need one replayable risk closure view.
new_state: Added reproducibility_risk_check.csv/json, verify_reproducibility_risk_check.py, and test_reproducibility_risk_check.py; neutralized meta-oriented path names in reviewer-facing evidence.
rationale: Close reproducibility-risk attack surfaces without changing primary evaluation denominators.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; aggregate verifier and unit-test counts increase through the reproducibility-risk gate.

old_state: Dependency provenance, module importability, denominator accounting, and content residue need executable checks.
new_state: Added dependency_provenance_gate, module_import_gate, denominator_integrity_gate, content_residue_gate, and expanded delivery/research-quality gates.
rationale: Close artifact-evaluator attack surfaces without changing primary evaluation denominators.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; aggregate verifier coverage expands through the new gates.

old_state: External-resource rows and runtime dependency license evidence need closed redistribution and provenance dispositions.
new_state: Added THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, resource_license_gate.csv/json, verify_resource_license_gate.py, and test_resource_license_gate.py; external_resources.csv records closed context/source/optional-backend dispositions.
rationale: Close license/provenance and external-resource interpretation attack surfaces without changing primary evaluation denominators.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; reproducibility-risk and research-quality gates include resource/license closure.

old_state: Replay should not depend on host PDF tooling, and prose counts must stay synchronized with claim ledgers.
new_state: Vendored pypdf, added import_surface_gate.csv/json and narrative_claim_scan_gate.csv/json with dedicated verifiers/tests, and synchronized README/reproduction count text.
rationale: Close host-dependency and prose-drift attack surfaces without changing primary evaluation denominators.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; aggregate verifier and unit-test coverage expand through import-surface and narrative-claim checks.

old_state: The main manuscript and reference ledger need locked research-facing presentation and bibliography dispositions.
new_state: Main-paper prose emphasizes the scientific method and evidence chain; manuscript_presentation_gate.csv/json and reference ledger locked dispositions are added.
rationale: Make the paper read as a top-tier research contribution while closing reference-ledger and presentation-style attack surfaces.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; manuscript presentation and reference-integrity gates are verifier-backed.

old_state: The package needs explicit data-availability and open-science/tool-use compliance evidence.
new_state: Added main-paper Data Availability text and open_science_compliance_gate.csv/json covering replay availability, privacy boundary, and disclosure requirements.
rationale: Close open-science and policy-compliance risks.
result_handling: Primary obligation/pass/failure counts are unchanged; verification covers the added compliance gate.

old_state: OPA/Rego can support counted claims only when the pinned executable is present and hash-matched before evaluation.
new_state: Added the hash-matched OPA v1.17.1 linux amd64 static executable, supplies precomputed reachable_roles to Rego, tightens denominator and adapter-reference gates for three counted adapters, cleans generated OPA fixture by-products before delivery checks, and reruns the complete verification chain.
rationale: Close external-validity concerns about having only Casbin and Cedar as counted adapters while preserving hash-gated execution discipline.
result_handling: Paper-facing artifact counts are recomputed over casbin_py, cedar_py, and opa_rego_cli: 15,840 applicable primary obligations, 15,840 passes, 3,240 rejected invalid transformations, 360 unsupported rows, and 15,840 adapter/reference agreement rows.
